_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to critically evaluate health information, services, and media messages about relationships, lifestyle choices, health, and wellbeing.

_source.w: We are learning to critically evaluate health information, services, and media messages about relationships, lifestyle choices, health, and wellbeing.
y: Critical evaluation protects us from misinformation that could harm our health and relationships.
Media literacy about health is essential in a world saturated with competing messages.
These skills empower us to make genuinely informed decisions about our own health and wellbeing.
eg: Critiquing a social media health trend and evaluating the evidence behind it.
Analysing how media messages about body image, alcohol, or relationships can be misleading.
Evaluating the quality of health services available to young people in our community.
Investigating how commercial interests influence health messaging.
Comparing health information from peer-reviewed sources with that from social media influencers.
Developing criteria for evaluating the credibility of health information and services.
